name: Hart County Bank And Trust Company
description_short: Hart County Bank and Trust Company is a community bank established in 1890, headquartered in Munfordville, Kentucky, with $23.4M in total assets and a focus on commercial lending.
description_long:
Hart County Bank and Trust Company has operated as a financial institution since February 20, 1890, making it a well-established community bank in Hart County, Kentucky. The bank is state-chartered, supervised by the FDIC (Certificate #10145), and held by Hart Bancshares Inc. It operates as a full-service brick-and-mortar banking office located at 331 Main Street in Munfordville, Kentucky 42765.

The bank offers traditional banking services typical of community financial institutions, including checking and savings accounts, deposit products, and commercial lending services. With total assets of $23.377 million and total deposits of $15.627 million, Hart County Bank specializes in commercial lending, positioning itself as a business-focused financial institution serving the local community. The bank maintains FDIC deposit insurance, protecting customer deposits up to applicable limits.

What distinguishes Hart County Bank is its deep local roots—operating in the same community for over 130 years—and its explicit specialization in commercial lending rather than consumer products. The bank's quarterly return on assets of 2% and quarterly return on equity of 8% demonstrate consistent profitability, though as a small regional institution, it lacks the breadth of services and digital infrastructure of larger national banks. The institution serves a concentrated market in Hart County and surrounding areas.

Hart County Bank is best suited for business owners and commercial borrowers seeking a local lender with long-standing community connections rather than consumers seeking comprehensive digital banking or extensive branch networks. Prospective customers should understand that as a small community bank, it may have limited online banking capabilities, fewer branch locations, and potentially less competitive rates on deposit products compared to larger financial institutions. The bank's strong equity capital position ($7.426 million) indicates financial stability.
services: Local financial services for Hart County residents
pros: Established in 1890 with 130+ years of community banking experience
Specializes in commercial lending for business customers
Strong equity capital of $7.426 million indicates financial stability
Located in downtown Munfordville at accessible Main Street address
Profitable operations with $524,000 annual net income
State-chartered bank with federal regulatory oversight
cons: Very small institution ($23.4M assets) with limited service scope compared to regional/national banks
Website provides no information about deposit rates, loan products, fees, or online banking capabilities
Commercial lending focus may limit consumer-friendly products and services
Single location in Munfordville limits accessibility for customers outside immediate area
No evidence of modern digital banking, mobile app, or comprehensive online services
best_for: Small business owners and commercial borrowers in Hart County seeking local lending relationships
Local Munfordville residents prioritizing community bank relationships over large-chain convenience
Business customers needing personalized service from decision-makers with local market knowledge
diagnosis: Hart County Bank and Trust Company is best for local business owners and commercial borrowers in the Munfordville/Hart County area seeking relationship-based lending from an established community financial institution. The main caveat is that this is a very small regional bank with no visible digital infrastructure, limited public information about consumer products, and geographic limitations that make it unsuitable for customers seeking robust online banking or multi-state branch access.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Hart County Bank and Trust Company: Kentucky community bank established 1890 with focus on commercial lending and $23.4M in total assets.
